_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for the five module overview pages. Drives module-1-overview.html through module-5-overview.html via render_modules.py.

card1.subBanner: Units 1–3 • Chapters 2 & 3 • All My Eggses Live in Texas
card1.intro_html: Welcome to Bat City Collective! Your first module establishes your development environment and introduces the foundations of Python programming. You’ll build the character creation screen for Deep in the Heart: A Lone Star Journey—the Texas road trip game you’ll develop all semester for our restaurant client.

labs.title: Welcome to San Antonio
labs.focus_html: Variables, input/output, f-strings. Build the character creation screen.

labs.title: Choose Your Destination
labs.focus_html: Decision structures (if/elif/else). Player picks a city and gets feedback.

labs.title: Hit the Road
labs.focus_html: Nested decisions, logical operators. Complete first trip with outcomes.

card2.topperTitle: Client Context: All My Eggses Live in Texas
card2.subBanner: What the Client Needs This Module
card2.intro_html: Bobby Hernandez (Director of Technology) is replacing the peg games on restaurant tables with tablet terminals. He wants a retro Texas road trip game that families can play while waiting for their chicken fried steak. Your job: build the first playable prototype.

mentor.name: Danny Cavanaugh
mentor.quote_html: “Before you build a castle, build a room. Before a room, build a wall. Before a wall, place one brick. That’s Module 1—we’re laying bricks.”

card3.end_state_html: By the end of Module 1, your game will: create a character, pick a destination from San Antonio, and show different outcomes based on the player’s choices—all deterministic, no randomness yet. It’s a complete experience, just simple.
card3.next_step_html: Start with Lab 1-1: Welcome to San Antonio in the Assignments section.

card4_recordings.intro_html: Recordings will be posted here after each Tuesday office hours session.

card1.subBanner: Units 4–6 • Chapters 4 & 5 • All My Eggses Live in Texas
card1.intro_html: In Module 1, you chose a destination—but never actually went anywhere! Time to hit the road. This module introduces loops and functions, transforming your static menu into a dynamic travel experience with randomness, hazards, and reusable code.

labs.title: Keep On Driving
labs.focus_html: While loops. Travel toward destination with fixed 50-mile turns.

labs.title: Texas Has Other Plans
labs.focus_html: Functions, random module. Create your own function library + add hazards.

labs.title: Road Trip Ready
labs.focus_html: Input validation, polished gameplay. The game loop is complete!

card2.subBanner: What the Client Needs This Module
card2.intro_html: Tammy Jo Hutchins-Miller (VP of Marketing) wants to see “something we can actually play” before the next stakeholder meeting. By Unit 6, you’ll have a complete gameplay loop: pick a city, travel there with random hazards, and see if you make it. That’s your demo.

mentor.name: Terrence “T-Bone” Booker
mentor.quote_html: “Now we add chaos. And I’m going to break EVERYTHING you just built. That’s my job. You’re welcome.”

card3.end_state_html: Player travels to cities via D20 distance rolls, faces random hazards with HEADS/TAILS coin flip outcomes. The game actually plays now. You’ve built your first game loop—and your first reusable function library.
card3.next_step_html: Start with Lab 2-1: Keep On Driving in the Assignments section.

card4_recordings.intro_html: Recordings will be posted here after each Tuesday office hours session.

card1.subBanner: Units 7–9 • Chapters 6 & 7 • All My Eggses Live in Texas
card1.intro_html: The game plays—but it doesn’t remember. This module introduces file I/O and lists so your game can save progress, track history, and show end-game statistics. Persistence and organization make games feel real.

labs.title: Where Have We Been?
labs.focus_html: File reading/writing basics. Save and load player progress.

labs.title: Save Your Progress
labs.focus_html: Lists and parallel arrays. Track visited cities and hazards encountered.

labs.title: Trip Report
labs.focus_html: Exception handling, list processing. Generate end-game stats report.

card2.subBanner: What the Client Needs This Module
card2.intro_html: Emmett Hutchins III (CEO) wants to see a leaderboard on the tablets: “Families love competition. Show ’em the best trips—fewest turns, most hazards survived, longest routes.” That means your game needs to save data, load data, and process it into meaningful stats.

mentor.name: Danny Cavanaugh
mentor.quote_html: “Always close what you open. Files. Doors. Conversations with your ex.”

card3.end_state_html: Code uses files to save/load data. Lists track visited cities and hazards. End-game stats appear: total miles, turns taken, hazards encountered. The game has memory—and players have bragging rights.
card3.next_step_html: Start with Lab 3-1: Where Have We Been? in the Assignments section.

card4_recordings.intro_html: Recordings will be posted here after each Tuesday office hours session.

card1.subBanner: Units 10–12 • Chapters 8 & 9 • All My Eggses Live in Texas
card1.intro_html: Your game works—but what if you had to add 20 more cities? This module introduces dictionaries, sets, and string methods for data-driven design. Stop hardcoding. Start scaling.

labs.title: City Data
labs.focus_html: Dictionaries. Store city info (name, distance, connections) as structured data.

labs.title: Flexible Input
labs.focus_html: String methods. Parse and validate user input with .strip(), .lower(), etc.

labs.title: Data-Driven Texas
labs.focus_html: Sets for unique tracking. Load cities from external data. Refactored game.

card2.topperTitle: Scalability Check: “What If We Add New Mexico?”
card2.subBanner: What the Client Needs This Module
card2.intro_html: Bobby Hernandez (Director of Technology) is already thinking about franchise expansion: “If we add 20 more cities, how long would it take? That’s your answer for whether this code is production-ready.” Time to make your game data-driven instead of hardcoded.

mentor.name: Priya Venkatesh
mentor.quote_html: “If you had to add 20 more cities, how long would it take? Five minutes or five hours? That answer tells you everything about your code quality.”

card3.end_state_html: Cities are dictionaries with rich data. String methods parse and validate input cleanly. Sets track unique items without duplicates. Adding a new city? One dictionary entry. That’s scalable code.
card3.next_step_html: Start with Lab 4-1: City Data in the Assignments section.

card4_recordings.intro_html: Recordings will be posted here after each Tuesday office hours session.

card1.subBanner: Units 13–15 • Chapter 10 • All My Eggses Live in Texas
card1.intro_html: You’ve been building a game all semester. Now you’re building a codebase. This module introduces classes and objects—the same game, but built like professionals build games. Welcome to Object-Oriented Programming.

labs.title: The City Class
labs.focus_html: Classes and objects. Convert city dictionaries into proper City objects.

labs.title: Deep in the Heart
labs.focus_html: Encapsulation. The Player object tracks all journey state—no more loose variables.

card2.subBanner: What the Client Needs This Module
card2.intro_html: Emmett Hutchins III is ready to roll out the tablets. Tammy Jo wants a photo op. Bobby has the deployment plan ready. Your game—Deep in the Heart: A Lone Star Journey—is about to go live in 47 Texas restaurants. Make it shine.

mentor.name: Sasha Mireles
mentor.quote_html: “You’ve been building a game all semester. Now you’ve built a codebase. That’s the difference between a hobby and a career.”

card3.end_state_html: City class, Game class, clean architecture. The same game you’ve been building—now built like a professional. Objects manage their own state. Methods do one thing well. The code is ready to hand off to the next developer. That’s shipping.

callout.title: You Made It!
callout.body_html: Lab 5-2 is the final assignment of the semester. When you submit, you’re not just completing an assignment—you’re shipping a product. Congratulations, developer.

card3.next_step_html: Start with Lab 5-1: The City Class in the Assignments section.

card4_recordings.intro_html: Recordings will be posted here after each Tuesday office hours session.
